title: Dryopteris subspinulosa
intro: Dryopteris subspinulosa, also known as the Chinese shield fern, is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in Chinese medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Dryopteris subspinulosa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and polysaccharides, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Dryopteris subspinulosa is commonly prepared as a decoction, tea, or tincture, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic benefits.
taxonomy: Dryopteris subspinulosa belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae. This species is classified as a type of fern, specifically within the genus Dryopteris. It is native to China and is a part of the larger plant kingdom known as the Plantae. The classification is further divided into the kingdom Plantae, phylum Pteridophyta, class Polypodiopsida, order Polypodiales, and family Dryopteridaceae. At the species level, Dryopteris subspinulosa is a distinct and unique taxon.
